context_files_note: Accuracy dimension is NOT scored by an LLM — it's derived from the regex validator + doc-as-test results we already produce. See Part B.
instructions:
Combine the 5 LLM-judge dimension scores + the accuracy dimension (derived from regex+doc-as-test, no LLM needed) into a per-page weighted overall score. Pure math + rendering — no LLM call.

## Part A — Aggregation script (`aggregate.py`)

Reads all 5 `<dimension>-latest.json` files from `results/`. For each page common to all five, computes an overall score as a weighted sum per `.docs-ops/rubric.json` `dimensions.<dim>.weight`:

  accuracy:              0.25
  completeness:          0.15
  cross_platform_parity: 0.20
  examples:              0.15
  clarity:               0.10
  ai_consumability:      0.15
  ---------------------  ----
  total:                 1.00

## Part B — Accuracy dimension (derived, not LLM-scored)

The accuracy dimension is the regex validator + doc-as-test output we already produce. Derive a per-page accuracy score (1-5) per these rules:

  - Start at 5.
  - Subtract 2 if the page appears in any regex drift report (`.docs-ops/evals/*-accuracy-drift.json`) with any flagged issue.
  - Subtract 2 if the page has any blocking failure in any doc-as-test report (`.docs-ops/integration-tests/*/results/latest.json`).
  - Subtract 1 if the page has any warning in doc-as-test reports.
  - Floor at 1.

Current state: all 4 platforms at regex drift=0 and doc-as-test 100% pass+skip, so most pages will score 5 on accuracy. Pages with warnings (iOS doc-as-test has 66 warnings, mostly preamble-style) drop to 4. Good ground-truth zero point.

## Part C — Per-page overall record schema

`results/overall-latest.json`:

```json
{
  "generated_at": "<iso>",
  "rubric_version": "0.1.0",
  "weights": { "accuracy": 0.25, "completeness": 0.15, "cross_platform_parity": 0.20, "examples": 0.15, "clarity": 0.10, "ai_consumability": 0.15 },
  "pages_scored": 10,
  "avg_overall": 3.91,
  "distribution": { "1": 0, "2": 0, "3": 2, "4": 7, "5": 1 },
  "by_page": [
    {
      "page": "social-plus-sdk/getting-started/authentication.mdx",
      "overall": 3.65,
      "per_dimension": {
        "accuracy": { "score": 5, "source": "derived" },
        "clarity": { "score": 3, "source": "llm-judge" },
        "cross_platform_parity": { "score": 3, "source": "llm-judge" },
        "completeness": { "score": 3, "source": "llm-judge" },
        "examples": { "score": 4, "source": "llm-judge" },
        "ai_consumability": { "score": 3, "source": "llm-judge" }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Round `overall` to 2 decimal places. Show floor of overall as the integer-distribution bucket.

## Part D — Renderer (`render-report.py` extension)

Extend the existing render-report.py with `--overall` mode that reads `overall-latest.json` and produces `results/overall-latest-report.md`:

  - Headline: avg overall + distribution histogram
  - Per-page table sorted by overall ascending (worst first)
  - Per-dimension column showing each score per page (heat-map-style ASCII: ✓ for 4-5, ~ for 3, ✗ for 1-2)
  - 'Action priority' section: top-3 pages by lowest overall, with dimension-breakdown so you know WHERE to invest per page

Keep it plain markdown.

## Part E — First run + sanity check

Run aggregate.py. Verify:
  - All 10 pilot pages have valid overall scores in [1.0, 5.0]
  - Weights sum to 1.00
  - Accuracy derivation matches expectation (most pages = 5, iOS pages = 4 if they have doc-as-test warnings)
  - Overall scores roughly track per-dimension weighted average

Report in `notes`: per-page overall scores sorted worst-first; the 3 worst pages and their per-dimension breakdowns; any pages where the overall score surprised you given the per-dimension scores (e.g., one dimension dominating).

## Out of scope

- CI gate integration of overall scores — much later.
- Cross-page LLM 'should this page even exist' meta-judgment — different problem.
- Trend tracking — that's a dashboards extension (after this, can extend the dashboards layer to show overall-score trends).

validators.name: aggregate_runs_and_produces_overall_report
validators.command: test -f .docs-ops/rubric-scorer/results/overall-latest.json && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/rubric-scorer/results/overall-latest.json')); assert d.get('pages_scored',0)>=8; assert all(1.0<=p.get('overall',0)<=5.0 for p in d.get('by_page',[])); ws=d.get('weights',{}); assert abs(sum(ws.values())-1.0)<0.001, f'weights must sum to 1.00, got {sum(ws.values())}'; print(f'OK pages={d.get(\"pages_scored\")} avg_overall={d.get(\"avg_overall\"):.2f}')"
